message:
fix(http): add write_all_with_retry to handle transient Ok(0) from TLS transports

Add a write_all_with_retry() async helper that retries on Ok(0) returns
from poll_write with exponential backoff instead of immediately failing
with ErrorKind::WriteZero. This fixes spurious "IO error: write zero"
failures seen during large request body writes (e.g. resumed session
contexts):

- TLS transports can temporarily return Ok(0) when internal buffers are
  full, but standard write_all treats this as unrecoverable
- Retry up to 10 times with 10ms initial backoff (doubling each attempt)
- After exhausting retries, surface a descriptive WriteZero error with
  the consecutive zero count and remaining bytes

Constants:
- WRITE_ZERO_MAX_RETRIES = 10
- WRITE_ZERO_BACKOFF = 10ms initial

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
